name: Jigsaw

facts.founded: ~2019-2020 (disputed: Legal IT Insider 2019, Tracxn 2020; funding activity from Feb 2023)
facts.hq: London, UK (offices also cited in US and Singapore)
facts.total_funding: $25M total ($15M Series A Apr 2024 + ~$10M prior pre-seed/seed since Feb 2023)
facts.lead_investor: Exor Ventures (angels from Linklaters, Morgan Stanley, KKR)
facts.headcount: unknown (disputed: press/self-reported ~145-150, Tracxn 32 as of Jul 2024)
customers: unknown (only self-reported aggregate: '200+ top-tier law firms, financial institutions, asset managers'; no named logos confirmed)
facts.agentic_interop: No confirmed public API/MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent. Has 'Jigbot' AI assistant (builds/edits diagrams from email/text deal summaries) and computer-vision sketch capture. Interop is closed Office/iManage embedding, not an open developer surface.
facts.tech_model: AI/computer-vision diagramming engine converting text/email deal summaries, documents, or sketch photos into data-rich corporate structure diagrams, timelines and step plans stored as queryable data.
facts.pricing: unknown (no public pricing; enterprise/custom quote implied)
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = jigsawcreate.com (UK 'Jigsaw Tech Ltd'), corporate-structure diagramming, $15M Series A Apr 2024, founders Scanlan & Leon. Ruled out: Google Jigsaw (Alphabet), jigsaw.ai (data platform), JigsawStack (dev AI API), Jigsaw Trading (futures software), a16z Speedrun 'Jigsaw', CMU HCII research prototype. Website is jigsawcreate.com, NOT jigsaw.legal.

uniqueness.justification: Visual-first structure-diagramming-as-system-of-record is a genuinely differentiated niche with little direct competition (vs PowerPoint/Visio).

thesis_fit.justification: Lives in Office/iManage where lawyers work, but is an assistive diagramming app, not agentic/supervised-autonomy; Jigbot is a feature, no open agent surface.

investability.justification: $25M raised, Exor Ventures lead, marquee angels, repeat founders with prior exit (XRef->Litera); strong on paper despite unverified traction.

defensibility.justification: Structured-data model and CV capture create some moat, but no API/network effects and no named customer lock-in evidence; conservative given thin traction data.

interoperability.justification: Office and iManage embedding only; no confirmed API, MCP, CLI or agent-to-agent — closed integration surface.

team.justification: Repeat legal-tech founders (Scanlan, Leon) with prior XRef exit to Litera and deep BigLaw/Litera pedigree.

provisional_verdict: Differentiated diagramming-as-data play with strong backing and repeat founders, but closed integration surface and no agentic/interop depth limit thesis fit. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Headcount conflict (32 vs ~145-150) — material discrepancy
No named customers; '200+' claim is self-reported marketing
Founded-year conflict (2019 vs 2020 vs activity from 2023)
Founder bio inconsistency (Scanlan ex-Akin Gump per Law.com vs ex-Simmons & Simmons elsewhere)
No confirmed open API/MCP/CLI/agent interop — likely absent
RollOnFriday '£100M sale' refers to founders' earlier XRef chain, NOT a Jigsaw exit

sources.what_it_supports: $15M Series A Apr 2024, Exor lead, corporate diagramming, UK/US/Singapore offices, founders
Confirmed entity website, self-reported '200+ firms', integrations
Jigbot AI assistant, computer-vision sketch capture

name: Augmetec

facts.hq: London, UK (registered at 71-75 Shelton Street, a serviced/formation-agent address)
facts.lead_investor: Fuel Ventures (co-investor 14Peaks Capital; angels incl. retired Kirkland & Ellis senior partner, NetDocuments founders' family office). Pre-seed from Haatch Ventures.
facts.headcount: unknown (no credible figure; the '11-50' result was for wrong entity 'Augmetic'; likely <20 at seed stage)
customers: unknown (anonymized testimonials only: 'Top Ranked Law Firm', 'International Law Firm'; no named logos)
facts.agentic_interop: Marketed as an investigations 'command centre' but evidence of true agentic/agent-to-agent architecture is thin. LEIAA v2.0 does AI automation/workflows (auto-identify evidence, detect fraudulent docs, build chronologies, 'suggest team actions'). No public API/developer docs, no MCP, no CLI, no named autonomous agent found. Reads as assistive workflow AI, not agentic.
facts.tech_model: Secure single-interface SaaS that ingests investigation evidence/documents and applies AI (analytics, document/fraud detection, NLP for witness/interview prep, chronology building, automated reporting) over the investigation lifecycle. Model stack undisclosed.
facts.pricing: unknown (no public pricing; enterprise SaaS assumed)
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Augmetec (London legaltech, product 'LEIAA', uk.linkedin.com/company/augmetec). HIGH spelling-collision risk: ruled out Augmetic/Augmetic Technology, Augmetek, Augmedix (US healthcare AI), Augentic AI. Verify spelling 'Augmetec' + product 'LEIAA' on any data feed.

uniqueness.justification: Investigations-specific 'command centre' (witness prep, fraud detection, chronologies) is a defined niche, but crowded vs Relativity/ediscovery incumbents; differentiation is workflow packaging.

thesis_fit.justification: 'AI as preparation/accountability' framing fits well (investigation prep, defensible answers), and it integrates into Relativity/iManage/NetDocuments — but agentic/supervised-autonomy is unverified; 'suggest', not act.

investability.justification: Only ~£2.4M total, seed-stage, credible micro-VC (Fuel) + strategic angels, but very early and traction unverified.

defensibility.justification: Thin evidence of moat — workflow AI over third-party models, no named customers, no proprietary-data lock-in shown; conservative on thin evidence.

interoperability.justification: Real DMS/ediscovery connectors (Relativity, iManage, NetDocuments) are meaningful, but no API docs/MCP/CLI/A2A — integration is connector-level only.

team.justification: CEO Kritin Sundaram is ex-HSF/Fieldfisher investigations lawyer (domain-credible); other co-founders' backgrounds largely unverified.

provisional_verdict: Domain-credible investigations workflow AI with real DMS/ediscovery connectors, but very early, unfunded-deep, and 'command centre' agentic claims are unverified. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Very early stage (~£2.4M total); product young despite 'v2.0'
No named customers — anonymized testimonials only
Most substantive claims self-reported/PR (45% efficiency, ISO/SOC certs, '150+ investigations')
Agentic framing vs reality gap — no API/MCP/CLI or named agent verifiable
Headcount unconfirmed; registered at serviced-office address
Spelling collision risk (Augmetic/Augmetek/Augmedix/Augentic)
Founding-year discrepancy (2022 vs 2023)

sources.what_it_supports: Founded 2022, seed funding, regulatory investigations platform, integrations
Fuel Ventures lead, 14Peaks co-investor, strategic angels (PR/investor-self-interest)

name: Crimson

facts.hq: London, UK (New York office opened May 2026, led by ex-Patterson Belknap litigator Rhick Bose)
facts.total_funding: $2.5M seed (~£1.8M), 'oversubscribed' — NOTE: brief's '$25M' is wrong, a URL-slug artifact; every credible source says $2.5M
facts.lead_investor: Y Combinator (Spring 2025 cohort); plus Symphony Ventures, Twenty Two Ventures, ACQ Ventures, Amino Capital, Eight Capital, Scale Asia Ventures, Progressive Ventures, and legal-industry/arbitrator angels
facts.headcount: ~6 (London core team, per GetLatka estimate — treat as approximate/unverified)
customers: unknown (claims 'Magic Circle and Am Law 10 firms, arbitration practices, litigation boutiques'; no firm named)
facts.agentic_interop: Positioned as 'The AI Associate for Litigation Lawyers' doing multi-step case work, but no source confirms autonomous-agent/agent-to-agent architecture. No public API, no MCP, no CLI found. Integration is DMS connector-level (iManage/SharePoint) for ingestion. Agentic claims unverified.
facts.tech_model: Cloud-based vertical LLM application for litigation/arbitration — ingests case documents, extracts issues/entities/events, flags inconsistencies between party positions, produces citation-grounded answers and drafts (RAG-style grounding); foundation-model provider undisclosed.
facts.pricing: unknown (no public pricing; subscription/enterprise custom implied)
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = crimson.law, AI litigation/disputes platform, YC Spring 2025, co-founder Mark Feldner. Ruled out: Crimson Education (NZ admissions), Crimson Wine Group, The Harvard Crimson, Crimson cybersecurity vendors, Crimson Legal/Crimson Legal Group law firms. KEY CORRECTION: funding is $2.5M not $25M.

uniqueness.justification: Litigation/arbitration 'case intelligence' workspace is a defined wedge, but RAG-over-documents with citations is increasingly common; differentiation is litigation-native UX and founder credibility.

thesis_fit.justification: 'AI as preparation/accountability' fits (citation-grounded, matter-scoped); lives in iManage/SharePoint — but agentic/supervised-autonomy unverified and it reads as a vertical LLM app.

investability.justification: YC-backed, oversubscribed $2.5M seed, A&O Shearman Fuse selection, strong founder pedigree — but very small/early and metrics self-reported.

defensibility.justification: Thin moat — vertical LLM wrapper (RAG + citations) on third-party models, no proprietary data or network effects shown; conservative on thin evidence.

interoperability.justification: Only iManage + SharePoint confirmed; no Relativity, no Salesforce, no public API/MCP/CLI despite ediscovery framing.

team.justification: Strong: CEO Mark Feldner (ex-Clifford Chance/WilmerHale/Willkie litigator), CTO David Strömbäck (ex-Flutter head of data science leading 13 AI engineers).

provisional_verdict: Credible litigation-native founders and YC backing, but a tiny, very-early vertical LLM app with thin interop and self-reported traction. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Brief's funding figure is WRONG ($2.5M not $25M) — correct internal records
Very early/small: 2025-founded, ~6 people; servicing 'Magic Circle/Am Law 10' is a diligence stretch
No named customers; '$40bn disputes' and '30% MoM growth' are self-reported, unverified
Interop thin — only iManage+SharePoint; no Relativity/Salesforce/API/MCP/CLI
Tech is a vertical LLM wrapper on undisclosed foundation models
Third co-founder (Amine Amor) and GetLatka revenue figure are single-source/low-confidence

sources.what_it_supports: $2.5M seed dated 2026-05-28, New York office, Rhick Bose
YC lead, investor syndicate, A&O Shearman Fuse
Product capabilities (per-matter workspaces, document review, timelines)

name: Lawstronaut

facts.founded: unknown exact (~2024-2025; EUIPO trademark filed Feb 2025; parent filerskeepers founded 2018; surfaced publicly at ILTACON 2025)
facts.hq: Operationally Amsterdam, Netherlands (founders + filerskeepers base); trademark/contracting entity is 'Lawstronaut FZCO', a Dubai free-zone company
facts.total_funding: unknown (no round/amount/investor found; parent filerskeepers shows no funding data — likely bootstrapped)
facts.headcount: unknown for Lawstronaut standalone (parent filerskeepers listed 51-100; Lawstronaut team likely much smaller)
customers: unknown (parent filerskeepers names Alibaba, Heineken, Coca-Cola for data-retention, NOT confirmed as Lawstronaut API customers)
integrations: MCP server connecting to AI agents (e.g. Claude)
RESTful API (no DMS/Word/SharePoint/Salesforce connectors — it is a data layer, not a workflow app)
facts.agentic_interop: Strong infra-platform signals (all self-reported): RESTful legal-data API over 45M+ laws/regs/cases across 150+ jurisdictions, OAuth 2.0, JSON, versioned content; explicit MCP server that 'hands your agent the slice of law it needs, with provenance' (citation+timestamp+link in-turn); continuous ingestion ~2M records/day with article-level change detection. Genuinely a platform-others-build-on. No CLI or A2A framework found.
facts.tech_model: Continuously crawls official government legal sources across 150+ jurisdictions, normalizes into a versioned structured schema with article-level change detection, and serves the corpus to developers/AI agents via REST API and an MCP server with provenance/citations.
facts.pricing: Starter $15/month; Team $25 per user/month; Enterprise custom (self-reported from site)
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Lawstronaut (lawstronaut.com), legal-data infrastructure spun out of filerskeepers; founders Wanne Pemmelaar & Madeleine Vos. Trademark held by Lawstronaut FZCO (Dubai). No competing 'space/NASA' commercial entity. Operating team Amsterdam, contracting entity Dubai FZCO — flag for KYC.

uniqueness.justification: Legal-data-layer-for-builders (API + MCP with provenance across 150+ jurisdictions) is a genuinely differentiated infra play; few direct peers (CourtListener/Free Law Project, TR MCP).

thesis_fit.justification: Squarely on thesis — API/MCP, agent-native, provenance/accountability built in, lives where agents/builders work; the clearest 'lives where lawyers/agents work' fit of the cohort.

investability.justification: No confirmable funding, no standalone headcount, no named customers; essentially undiligenceable on financials at this stage.

defensibility.justification: Continuously-maintained normalized 150+-jurisdiction corpus with change detection is a real data moat IF the claims hold — but all self-reported and unbenchmarked; conservative.

interoperability.justification: Best-in-cohort: REST API (OAuth 2.0) + MCP server with provenance, explicitly agent-native; this IS the product.

team.justification: Founders Pemmelaar (ex-A&O/De Brauw/Stibbe, filerskeepers CEO) and Vos are credible data/tech lawyers with an established global data-retention track record (Chambers/Legal 500).

provisional_verdict: The most thesis-aligned of the cohort — a genuine agent-native legal-data API/MCP infra layer with credible founders — but earliest and thinnest on funding/traction evidence. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: No confirmable funding; likely bootstrapped/very early
No standalone Lawstronaut headcount or named customers
All capability metrics (45M+ records, 2M/day, 150+ jurisdictions) self-reported, unbenchmarked
Entity split: Amsterdam ops vs Dubai FZCO trademark/contracting entity — clarify KYC/contracting party
No Law.com/TechCrunch/Sifted coverage; only ILTA startup-hub + own site
No CLI or agent-to-agent framework (API + MCP only)

sources.what_it_supports: API/MCP product, 45M+ records/150+ jurisdictions, pricing (all self-reported)
ILTACON 2025 startup-hub profile, infra-platform positioning, MCP/data pipeline
Spun out of filerskeepers, founders Pemmelaar & Vos
ILTA Voices podcast July 2025, public surfacing date
Trademark held by Lawstronaut FZCO (Dubai entity)
EUIPO mark filed Feb 2025, Lawstronaut FZCO
Category context — MCP legal-data integrations with Claude
